bradford-county-fl-commission-approves-i: Bradford County FL commission approved an IGSA for ICE detention 3-2, citing a $5.5M budget shortfall. Sabot Consulting model using county-owned Douglas Building.
kemmerer-wy-city-council-rejects-sabot-9: Kemmerer WY city council rejected Sabot Consulting's 900-bed ICE detention pitch with Akima as operator. Community reaction was 'overwhelmingly no.'
romulus-mi-city-and-state-file-lawsuit-t: City of Romulus and Michigan AG filed suit Mar 2026 to block ICE warehouse-to-detention conversion. 700-800 protesters; Governor Whitmer notably silent.
romulus-mi-governor-whitmer-silent-while: Governor Whitmer stayed silent on the Romulus ICE warehouse while AG Nessel led opposition. Mirrors a national pattern of governors avoiding ICE confrontation.
social-circle-ga-walton-county-nda-cited: Walton County GA officials cited NDAs to withhold detention facility plans from the public. Matches Sabot's 'managing the noise' playbook.
ahtna-technical-services: Ahtna Technical Services is an Alaska Native Corp subsidiary using 8(a) sole-source contracting to operate ICE facilities including Port Isabel TX. ~$800M in historical contracts.
apex-site-services: Apex Site Services is a North Texas construction firm that pivoted from COVID testing sites to proposing soft-sided ICE detention facilities, including in Walsenburg CO.
babel-street: Babel Street sells ICE location tracking (Locate X) and social media monitoring (Babel X) using ad-tech data harvested from phone apps. No warrant required.
bi-incorporated: BI Incorporated (GEO Group subsidiary) runs ICE's ankle monitor and SmartLINK surveillance program for 180,000+ people, plus a $121M skip-tracing contract to hunt non-compliant targets.
capgemini-government-solutions: Capgemini won the largest ICE skip-tracing contract ($365M ceiling) to locate immigrants for deportation. The French multinational reportedly had internal doubts about reputational risk.
clearview-ai: Clearview AI provides ICE with facial recognition from a 50B+ image database via a $9.2M sole-source contract. Used with 'little apparent oversight' per WBEZ.
constellis-holdings: Constellis (Blackwater/Academi successor) hunts immigrants for ICE under a $113M skip-tracing contract. Operatives conduct physical surveillance with no DHS credentials.
corecivic: CoreCivic is the second-largest private prison company with $2.2B revenue in 2025, up 13%. Won ~$325M in new ICE contracts reactivating 6,353 beds across four facilities.
csi-aviation: CSI Aviation manages ICE Air deportation flights, tripling to $1.1B in FY2025. Founded by Republican donor Allen Weh; subcontracts to GlobalX and World Atlantic.
endeavors: Endeavors is a San Antonio nonprofit that received ~$3.2B in federal migrant services contracts under Biden. DOGE terminated a $215M contract for an empty Pecos facility.
g4s-secure-solutions: G4S (Allied Universal subsidiary, formerly Wackenhut) provides armed ground transportation of ICE detainees across multiple field offices. $100M San Diego contract solicited.
geo-group: GEO Group is the largest private detention company ($2.6B revenue, $254M profit in 2025) operating a closed loop: surveillance via BI, skip tracing, and 75,000 detention beds.
globalx-airlines: GlobalX Airlines operates ~80% of ICE deportation flights, earning $246M in 2025. Flight attendants reported shackled passengers cannot be safely evacuated in emergencies.
immigration-centers-of-america: Immigration Centers of America is a niche operator running the 732-bed Farmville VA detention center under an IGSA. Facility is notorious for abuse and inadequate medical care.
lexisnexis: LexisNexis sells ICE and CBP access to Accurint, a database of 10,000+ sources including utility records and license plates. Combined contracts worth up to $38M.
mtc: MTC is the third-largest private prison operator, pursuing a sole-source ICE contract for a shuttered Marana AZ prison. Pima County formally opposed it in Feb 2026.
mvm-inc: MVM Inc. is a security contractor founded by ex-Secret Service agents with $1.1B in cumulative ICE transport contracts. Owners are major Trump donors.
nice-systems: NICE Systems is an Israeli company providing phone recording, keyword search, and video surveillance to US prisons and jails. Specific ICE contract not yet confirmed.
palantir-technologies: Palantir builds ICE's core surveillance infrastructure: ImmigrationOS deportation pipeline, ELITE targeting app, and ICM case management. Total ICE contracts ~$287M.
sabot-consulting: Sabot Consulting markets 'ICE Integration' services to county sheriffs, staffed by former ICE officials including Acting Director Tae Johnson. Drives the county-led IGSA detention model.
shadowdragon: ShadowDragon provides ICE with social media surveillance across 200+ websites including dating sites, forums, and social platforms. ICE plans a $20-50M monitoring office expansion.
thomson-reuters: Thomson Reuters CLEAR gives ICE access to utility records, phone records, license plates, and real-time geolocation. $161M+ in cumulative DHS contracts since 2003.
venntel-gravy-analytics: Venntel sells ICE precise cell phone location data harvested from advertising bid-streams without warrants. FTC filed a complaint in Dec 2024 but ICE expanded purchases.
vigilant-solutions-motorola: Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) operates the largest US license plate reader network. ICE has a $22.8M database contract plus purchased mobile ALPR hardware for field operations.
world-atlantic-airways: World Atlantic Airways is a Miami-based overflow carrier for ICE deportation flights, subcontracting under CSI Aviation. Increased role after iAero Airways bankruptcy in 2023.
bi-incorporated-isap-v: $1B sole-source contract for BI Inc (GEO subsidiary) to run ICE's ISAP V ankle monitor and SmartLINK surveillance program. ICE targets 180,000 on GPS monitors by 2026.
bi-incorporated-skip-tracing: $121M contract for BI Inc to hunt immigrants who missed check-ins using its own ISAP surveillance data. Completes GEO Group's monitor-hunt-detain closed loop.
bradford-county-igsa-proposal: Sabot Consulting proposed a 3,000-bed phased IGSA detention facility in Bradford County FL worth $239M/year at full capacity. County commission voted 3-2 to advance.
camp-east-montana-1-2b-contract: $1.2B Army contract for a tent detention camp at Fort Bliss TX, awarded to a no-experience contractor at a Virginia residential address. Three deaths in first five months.
clearview-ai-ice-2025: $9.2M sole-source ICE contract with Clearview AI for facial recognition matching against a 50B+ image database. Justified for child exploitation and officer assault cases.
g4s-ground-transportation: G4S (Allied Universal) holds multiple ICE ground transportation task orders across six field offices. A $100M San Diego contract was solicited for FY2026.
geo-wexmac-titus-prequalification: GEO Group was quietly pre-qualified for WEXMAC-TITUS, giving it no-bid access to $45B in OBBBA detention funding. Announced on a Friday night in Feb 2026.
glades-county-igsa-2025: ICE reinstated the Glades County FL IGSA on April 3, 2025 for up to 500 beds operated by the sheriff. Sabot Consulting featured it as a client success story.
ice-air-csi-aviation-fy2025: CSI Aviation's ICE Air contract surged from $364M (FY2024) to $1.1B (FY2025). Domestic transfer flights jumped 132% as new remote detention facilities opened.
ice-skip-tracing-2025: $1.2B program across 13 private contractors to locate 1.5M immigrants for deportation using AI, databases, and physical surveillance. Operatives carry no DHS credentials.
lexisnexis-leids-ice: $38M combined ICE ($22.1M) and CBP ($15.9M) contracts for LexisNexis LEIDS database access including Accurint dossiers and license plate recognition.
mvm-inc-transportation: $1.1B cumulative MVM Inc. contracts for ICE detainee and unaccompanied child transportation. $145M in new 2025 contracts; owner family are major Trump donors.
palantir-icm-2022: $139.3M Palantir contract for ICE's Investigative Case Management system (2022-2026). Slated for sole-source renewal because no comparable system exists.
palantir-immigrationos-2025: $59.9M sole-source Palantir contracts to build ImmigrationOS, ICE's deportation pipeline platform with AI targeting, confidence scoring, and self-deportation tracking.
thomson-reuters-clear-ice: $22.8M five-year DHS contract for Thomson Reuters CLEAR database access. 2025 option confirms license plate reader data provision alongside utility and phone records.
vigilant-alpr-ice: $22.8M ICE contract for Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) license plate reader database access through 2026. ICE also purchased mobile ALPR hardware for ERO field operations.
bradford-county-fl-douglas-building: Community in Bradford County FL contested a 3,000-bed IGSA facility on a VOC-contaminated site pitched by Sabot Consulting. Commission voted 3-2 to advance; lease on agenda before environmental studies complete.
durant-ok-choctaw-nation: Choctaw Nation purchased a Durant OK warehouse targeted by DHS, directly blocking ICE acquisition. Novel use of tribal sovereignty and economic power to remove property from federal reach.
hanover-va-terminated: Warehouse owners in Hanover County VA terminated their deal with ICE after community pressure made the sale politically toxic. Part of a pattern of seller-side resistance.
kansas-city-mo-platform-ventures: Community in Kansas City blocked a 7,500-bed mega center through student walkouts, mass protests, and a general strike. Platform Ventures withdrew Feb 12, 2026 after Port KC severed ties.
merrimack-nh-paused: Community in Merrimack NH blocked an ICE processing center. The fight produced leaked ICE planning documents revealing the 34-facility Detention Reengineering Initiative for the first time.
palm-beach-fl-belly-of-the-beast: Palm Beach County FL leads Florida in immigration arrests with 118 designated immigration officers and 1,400+ arrests since Aug 2025. Student walkouts and community resistance centered in Lake Worth Beach.
pinal-county-az-rogue-attorney: County Attorney Brad Miller signed a 287(g) with ICE without board approval; Board unanimously declared it illegal and sued. Active litigation in Maricopa County Superior Court as of April 2026.
romulus-mi-warehouse-fight: Michigan AG Nessel and City of Romulus sued to block an ICE warehouse purchased without notice near schools and in a floodplain. 700+ protesters rallied; Gov. Whitmer notably silent.
roxbury-nj-lawsuit: NJ Governor, AG, and Roxbury Township jointly sued to block a 470K sq ft ICE warehouse. Project NINJA organized 1,500+ community members -- broadest multi-level legal opposition of any facility fight.
salt-lake-city-ut-mega-center-fight: Community in Salt Lake City contested a $145.4M mega center for 7,500-10,000 beds. City imposed water restrictions following Social Circle model; purchase already completed limits options.
shakopee-mn-rejected: Warehouse owner in Shakopee MN declined to sell to ICE after community opposition made the deal politically toxic. Follows the Kansas City seller-pressure model.
social-circle-ga-water-shutoff: Social Circle GA locked the water meter on a planned 10,000-bed mega center because its 1M gal/day sewage demand exceeded the city's 660K gal/day capacity. Tactic replicated in Salt Lake City.
surprise-az-ban: Surprise AZ passed a 5-year ban on detention facilities after DHS bought a $70M+ warehouse without notice. Ban is unenforceable due to federal preemption -- illustrates limits of post-purchase resistance.
2026-03-24-mullin-pause-warehouse-purcha: DHS Secretary Mullin paused 11 ICE warehouse conversions and imposed $25M contract approval threshold on March 24, 2026, reviewing all Noem-era contracts after $364M in documented overpayments.
2026-03-26-dhs-ig-investigation-noem-lew: DHS IG launched investigation into Noem/Lewandowski contract handling on March 26, 2026. Lewandowski allegedly solicited success fees from GEO Group; contract corruption was a catalyst for Noem's firing.
2026-03-30-congressional-investigation-w: 54 Congressional Democrats led by Raskin and Warren launched investigation into ICE warehouse profiteering on March 30, 2026. Documented 333-1000% markups and 33 admin members with Blue Owl investments.
alexandria-la-staging-facility: 400-bed GEO Group facility on a former Air Force Base tarmac in Alexandria LA, cycling 1,000+ people weekly. Cornerstone of ICE deportation flight operations and largest ICE Air hub.
baker-county-fl-detention: 1,300+ bed county jail in Sanderson FL (former state prison), opened Aug 2025 with GEO Group management. Part of Florida's IGSA detention network; dubbed 'Deportation Depot' by protesters.
bradford-county-douglas-building: Proposed 3,000-bed IGSA facility in Starke FL on a contaminated 30-acre site, phased from 1,000 to 3,000 beds at $221-269/bed/day. Pitched by Sabot Consulting as county-led economic development.
broward-transitional-center-fl: 600-bed GEO Group facility in Pompano Beach FL running at 105-108% capacity with $42.9M/year contract. 911 calls doubled in 2025; one death in custody; 80% of detainees have no criminal record.
california-city-ca-corecivic: 2,560-bed CoreCivic facility in Kern County CA generating ~$100M/year under a two-year ICE contract. One of the largest private detention centers in the country, reactivated Aug 2025.
camp-atterbury-in-military: Indiana National Guard base ~40 miles south of Indianapolis designated for up to 1,000 ICE detainees. Named in Defense Secretary Hegseth's July 2025 letter; still in planning phase.
camp-blanding-fl: Florida National Guard base near Starke planned for 2,000 ICE detainees, branded 'Deportation Depot' by DeSantis. Paused until Alligator Alcatraz fills up; state-operated model.
camp-east-montana-fort-bliss: 3,000+ bed tent camp on Fort Bliss in El Paso TX, opened Aug 2025 under a $1.2B Army contract. Three deaths in custody, tuberculosis cases, and documented abuse; operator being replaced.
dade-collier-alligator-alcatraz: 3,000-5,000 bed state-operated facility built in 8 days in the Florida Everglades, opened July 2025. Documented abuse, maggot-infested food, and legal challenges; costs $450M/year to operate.
delaney-hall-newark-nj: 1,000-bed GEO Group facility in Newark NJ reactivated in 2025 under a ~$1B contract. Part of GEO's expansion from 20,000 to 26,000 ICE beds driving record $254M profit in 2025.
el-paso-tx-eastwind-mega-center: $123M warehouse purchase in Far East El Paso County planned as 8,500-bed mega center. Three warehouse buildings totaling ~888K sq ft; faces water infrastructure limits.
eloy-detention-center-pinal-az: 1,550-bed CoreCivic facility in Eloy AZ called the 'deadliest ICE detention center' with 16 deaths including 5 suicides. Part of the 11,200+ bed Pinal County CoreCivic cluster.
florence-spc-pinal-az: ICE-operated processing center in Florence AZ with guard/food services contracted to Akima Global (Alaska Native Corp) at $37.4M/year via sole-source 8(a) contracts escalating 12.5% over 3 years.
folkston-ipc-charlton-ga: 3,000-bed GEO Group facility (merged with D. Ray James) in Charlton County GA -- nation's largest ICE detention center. 16 deaths, systemic violations, and economic capture of a 12,000-person county.
glades-county-fl-detention: 500-bed county jail in Moore Haven FL with IGSA reinstated April 2025. Part of Florida's county jail detention network; a 19-year-old detainee death drew media attention.
guantanamo-bay-migrant-operations: Trump ordered GMOC expansion from 130 to 30,000 capacity in Jan 2025, but fewer than 400 held at any time. Places immigration detainees outside normal federal court reach at estimated $40M/month.
hagerstown-md-warehouse: ICE purchased a Hagerstown MD warehouse for $100M+ in Jan 2026 as one of 16 regional processing centers. Part of the $38.3B Detention Reengineering Initiative targeting FY2026 completion.
hamburg-pa-processing-center: Warehouse in Berks County PA purchased as one of 16 ICE regional processing centers with 1,000-1,500 bed capacity. Part of the Pennsylvania detention cluster with Upper Bern and Tremont.
jbmdl-nj-military: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst in NJ approved for up to 1,000 ICE detainees per Hegseth's July 2025 letter. Governor Murphy opposed; no firm timeframe. NJ faces double expansion with Roxbury warehouse.
jena-la-central-processing: 1,160-bed GEO Group processing center in Jena LA, part of the Louisiana detention hub with Alexandria Staging Facility. Notable for holding detained college students including Mahmoud Khalil.
kansas-city-mo-mega-center: Planned 7,500-bed mega center in south Kansas City blocked when Platform Ventures withdrew after mass protests, student walkouts, and Port KC severing ties. Key seller-pressure resistance model.
la-palma-correctional-pinal-az: 3,060-bed CoreCivic facility in Eloy AZ converted from California state prisoners to ICE use in 2019. DHS OIG found abuse and mistreatment; staff deployed chemical agents on COVID protesters in 2020.
leavenworth-ks-corecivic: 1,033-bed former federal prison in Leavenworth KS awarded to CoreCivic via no-bid contract. Delayed by zoning challenge; excluded from CoreCivic's 2026 financial projections due to uncertainty.
merrimack-nh-warehouse: 43-acre warehouse in Merrimack NH planned as 500-1,500 bed ICE processing center at $300M estimated cost. Paused after community opposition in this swing-state town; ACLU obtained leaked ICE site plans.
north-lake-baldwin-mi: 1,800-bed GEO Group facility in Baldwin MI reactivated in 2025. Described as 'notorious' for conditions; part of GEO's expansion adding 6,000 beds to its ICE capacity.
romulus-mi-warehouse: 500-bed ICE warehouse at 7525 Cogswell St, Romulus MI purchased Feb 2026 without public notice. Near schools and in a floodplain; Michigan AG and city filed lawsuit to block conversion.
roxbury-nj-warehouse: $129.3M warehouse (470K sq ft) in Roxbury NJ purchased at 137% markup from Goldman Sachs subsidiary. Governor, AG, and township jointly sued; bipartisan backlash in NJ.
salt-lake-city-ut-mega-center: $145.4M purchase of 833K sq ft warehouse in Salt Lake City for 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. Sold by Deutsche Bank subsidiary at 50%+ markup; city imposed water restrictions to resist.
san-antonio-tx-warehouse: $66M+ warehouse (640K sq ft) on San Antonio's East Side planned as 1,500-bed ICE processing center. Assessed value was $37M; part of the 24-warehouse Detention Reengineering Initiative.
social-circle-ga-mega-center: $129M warehouse in Social Circle GA (valued at $29M, 333% markup) planned as 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. City locked water meter because 1M gal/day sewage demand exceeds town's 660K capacity.
socorro-tx-warehouse: ICE warehouse in Socorro, TX (El Paso County) purchased for $123M despite $11M prior valuation, a ~1000% markup and the most extreme overpayment documented in the ICE warehouse program.
surprise-az-warehouse: ICE warehouse in Surprise, AZ (Maricopa County), 550 beds, purchased for $70M vs $12M prior value (~500% markup). Contractor awarded the project has no detention background.
tremont-pa-mega-center: Former Big Lots warehouse in Tremont, PA (Schuylkill County), 7,500-10,000 beds, bought from Blue Owl Capital for $119M (~2x market value). 33 Trump officials hold Blue Owl investments.
upper-bern-pa-warehouse: ICE processing center in Upper Bern Township, PA (Berks County), purchased for $87M in Feb 2026. Legal questions raised about local authority to block federal detention facilities.
webb-county-detention-center-tx: CoreCivic-operated ICE detention facility in Laredo, TX under IGSA, on the I-35 corridor at the busiest US-Mexico commercial crossing. ANC score of 3 signals heavy alternatives-to-detention contracting.
williamsport-md-warehouse: Vacant warehouse in Williamsport, MD, 1,500 beds, owned by Fundrise and refinanced by Goldman Sachs months before DHS purchase. Judge extended restraining order against operations.
blue-owl-33-admin-investors: 33 Trump administration officials hold Blue Owl Capital investments per ProPublica disclosures, including Trump ($5M+) and SecNav Phelan, whose command administers WEXMAC-TITUS.
comprehensive-conflict-of-interest-map: Cross-reference of all 11 ICE warehouse sellers against Trump official financial disclosures. GSA Admin Forst's former employers Goldman and Deutsche Bank sold $274.7M in warehouses.
full-pipeline-conflict-map: Maps financial conflicts across all 7 stages of the ICE pipeline, from Palantir targeting (142 officials) to detention ops. Every stage has officials with financial ties to contractors.
geo-group-admin-financial-ties: GEO Group ties to Trump officials: Homan received GEO Care consulting fees as Border Czar, Venturella went from 12 years at GEO to running ICE detention contracting with ethics waiver.
goldman-352m-loan-portfolio: Goldman Sachs and TPG refinanced Fundrise's 12-property industrial portfolio for $352.7M in June 2025. Only Williamsport, MD was sold to DHS; remaining properties fit future ICE targets.
homan-full-portfolio-conflicts: Border Czar Tom Homan holds financial ties spanning every ICE contractor category: GEO Care fees, LexisNexis/RELX stock, CBRE stock, Prologis stock, and Blackstone stock.
lutnick-newmark-conflict: Commerce Secretary Lutnick held $80M+ in Newmark stock for 8 months after confirmation while Newmark brokered ICE warehouse deals. Son Kyle now sits on Newmark board.
palantir-admin-financial-ties: 142 Trump officials have Palantir financial ties, the largest conflict footprint of any detention contractor. Includes revolving-door hires at HHS, State, DOD, and both OMB procurement chiefs.
rogers-cbre-jll-loan-receivables: Asst SecNav Rogers has loan receivables from CBRE and JLL (both brokered ICE warehouse deals) plus Blue Owl stock, while his department administers WEXMAC-TITUS procurement.
sec-filings-fundrise-rreef: SEC filing forensics on Fundrise (Williamsport MD) and RREEF/Deutsche Bank (SLC UT) warehouse ownership chains. Fundrise investors lost 33% while property was sold to DHS.
warehouse-profiteering-pattern: Documents how ICE warehouse purchases function as wealth transfers: institutional investors buy distressed properties, DHS purchases at enormous markups via WEXMAC-TITUS, extinguishing bank debt with taxpayer funds.
287g-explosion-and-bounty-system: 287(g) grew 641% from 135 to 1,637 agreements by April 2026. ICE pays performance bonuses per immigration arrest, creating a self-reinforcing cycle feeding the detention pipeline.
coast-guard-ice-flights: At least 324 Coast Guard flights diverted to ICE detainee transport in 2025, using C-27J Spartans and C-130s as feeders for Air Force deportation flights from hub airports.
foia-requests-needed: Prioritized list of FOIA and state open records requests targeting NDAs with local officials, missing appraisals, WEXMAC-TITUS scope modifications, and Blue Owl ethics determinations.
ice-contract-pipeline: Framework mapping the 7-stage ICE enforcement pipeline from targeting to deportation, each served by different contractors. GEO Group operates a closed loop across surveillance, skip-tracing, and detention.
ice-detention-medical-care-crisis: 32 people died in ICE custody in 2025, the deadliest year in two decades. ICE stopped paying medical providers in Oct 2025, creating a ~$300M gap as detained population surged 82%.
igsa-model-shift-warehouse-pause-acceler: The April 2026 warehouse purchase pause is accelerating the county-led IGSA model, where sheriffs partner with Sabot Consulting to build detention facilities, bypassing federal purchase opposition.
military-infrastructure-three-uses: Military installations serve three distinct detention functions: active-base detention (avoiding civilian oversight), former-base staging facilities, and ICE Air deportation flight hubs.
resistance-tactics-what-works: Analysis of 12+ community fights showing what stops ICE facilities: pressure sellers before purchase, control municipal water/sewer, state AG lawsuits, environmental challenges, and emergency city council action.
tracking-resources: Curated list of external resources for monitoring detention expansion: facility maps (Courier, Freedom for Immigrants), flight monitors (Human Rights First), investigative journalists, and FOIA tools.
wexmac-titus-military-procurement-bypass: WEXMAC-TITUS is a Navy contracting vehicle repurposed for ICE detention, expanded from $10B to $65B ceiling. Bypasses competitive bidding, appraisals, and transparency required under normal GSA procurement.
blue-owl-capital: Blue Owl Capital ($307.5B AUM) sold distressed warehouses to DHS at multiples of market value. Stock crashed 68% amid $5.4B in redemption requests; DHS sales were a financial lifeline.
carlyle-group-detention: Carlyle Group sold the Oakwood, GA warehouse to DHS for $68.2M. 22 Trump officials hold Carlyle investments, including former Carlyle employee Todd Newnam now at Treasury.
goldman-sachs-detention: Goldman Sachs has double ICE exposure: direct seller of Roxbury NJ ($129.3M, 137% markup) and lender on Williamsport MD ($352.7M refi). Combined Goldman-connected proceeds: $231.7M.
pnk-group: Russia-founded industrial developer that sold Social Circle, GA warehouse to DHS for $128.6M after buying it for $29.4M. Founder Sharkov has Sberbank, Roscongress, and ICIJ offshore ties.
project-salt-box: Activist research org documenting ICE warehouse overpayments through property records and lien filings. Their data shows markups ranging from 137% to ~1000% across confirmed purchases.
rockefeller-group: Mitsubishi Estate subsidiary that sold the Surprise, AZ warehouse to DHS for $70M vs ~$12M cost basis (483% markup). GardaWorld won the $313.4M conversion contract despite no detention experience.
antoine-mccord: DHS CIO Antoine McCord is a former Anduril employee holding active Anduril equity. Paired with ex-Palantir OMB CIO Barbaccia, both Thiel-network alumni control federal tech procurement.
daniel-bible: Former ICE ERO deputy director who left days before the 2024 election to become GEO Group EVP. GEO invested $70M in detention expansion weeks after his hire, suggesting advance knowledge.
daniel-ragsdale: Former ICE Deputy Director (2012-2017) who joined GEO Group as EVP two months after leaving government. Now SVP for Contract Administration and Compliance at GEO.
david-venturella: Spent 12 years at GEO Group ($6M+ pay), now runs ICE detention contracting with an ethics waiver. Since his return, GEO won $520M in new contracts and reported 700% profit increase.
edward-dalelio: Blue Owl Capital director who previously served on the Trump Entertainment Resorts board, connecting the private equity firm profiting from ICE warehouses to the Trump business network.
edward-forst: GSA Administrator and former Goldman Sachs partner whose agency's procurement role was bypassed via WEXMAC-TITUS. All $1.074B in warehouse purchases closed after his Dec 2025 swearing-in.
john-mcgranahan: Former GSA General Counsel (through Nov 2025) who held Blue Owl Capital investments while at the agency that normally brokers federal real estate. Blue Owl sold Tremont PA for $119M.
matthew-albence: Former Acting ICE Director (2019-2020) who joined GEO Group as SVP Client Relations. Known for calling family detention centers 'more like summer camp.' One of 6+ ex-ICE officials at GEO.
tae-johnson: Former Acting ICE Director (31 years ICE/INS) now at Sabot Consulting, advising counties on building IGSA detention facilities. GAO found his service violated the Federal Vacancies Reform Act.
ice-detention-compliance-operations-cons: Sabot Consulting job posting for ICE detention compliance consultant in Florida (hybrid), requiring NDS 2025 expertise. Signals active Florida IGSA pipeline development.
Sabot Consulting job posting for ICE detention compliance consultant in Laredo, TX (Webb County). Webb County already has an IGSA, suggesting facility expansion or new site.
project-manager-detention-corrections: Sabot Consulting national job posting for detention and corrections project manager. Indicates active construction pipeline for IGSA detention facilities.
sabot-ice-detention-consultant-fort-laud: Sabot Consulting posting for ICE detention consultant in Fort Lauderdale (Broward County), with 'facility activation' language indicating a facility is being prepared for ICE use.
sabot-laredo-tx-posting-removed: Sabot Consulting removed its Laredo TX detention project manager posting, suggesting the role was filled. Signals active Sabot operations in Webb County, a border county with 2 IGSAs.
sabot-project-manager-doj-remote: Sabot Consulting hiring remote Project Manager for DOJ work. Signals expansion beyond ICE into Bureau of Prisons and USMS detention contracts.
new-mexico-hb-9-immigrant-safety-act-bil: New Mexico HB 9 would ban public bodies from entering ICE detention IGSAs. NM has 18 IGSA facilities, third highest nationally; bill signals active pipeline.
10801-nw-97th-st-miami-dade-fl-162000-sf: 162K SF warehouse at Flagler Station in Miami-Dade, owned by AEW Capital Management, currently leased. Part of Miami Trading Zone industrial cluster.
11301-nw-97th-ave-miami-dade-fl-174400-s: 174K SF warehouse in Medley, Miami-Dade, owned by Hilco Redevelopment Partners, leased to Garland Food. Standard industrial property.
12805-nw-42nd-ave-miami-dade-fl-235900-s: 236K SF Prologis distribution center in Opa-Locka, Miami-Dade, leased to Amazon. Active logistics property.
13606-furman-rd-harris-tx-183000-sf: 183K SF available warehouse in Houston, Harris County TX, divisible to 40K SF. Owned by Vault Distribution Center.
1511-zoo-pkwy-duval-fl-250000-sf: 250K SF Walmart distribution center in Jacksonville, Duval County FL, serving 18 Sam's Clubs. Active retail logistics site.
15810-walgreens-dr-palm-beach-fl-182800-: 183K SF warehouse in Jupiter, Palm Beach FL, owned by Ambrose Property Group, leased to Niagara Bottling. Standard industrial property.
3595-nw-125th-st-miami-dade-fl-229300-sf: 229K SF warehouse in Miami-Dade owned by TIAA, leased to Performance Food Group on renewal. Active distribution property.
4300-bulls-bay-hwy-duval-fl-262750-sf: 263K SF available FDA-registered food-grade warehouse in Jacksonville, Duval FL. Owned by Total Distribution Inc.
5601-nw-72nd-ave-miami-dade-fl-364600-sf: 365K SF warehouse in Miami-Dade owned by Clarion Partners, leased to CEVA Logistics. Airport North/Medley submarket.
600-ellis-rd-n-duval-fl-200000-sf: 200K SF available temperature-controlled FDA-registered warehouse in Jacksonville, Duval FL. Owned by Total Distribution Inc.
7000-nw-32nd-ave-miami-dade-fl-162100-sf: 162K SF distribution center in Miami-Dade owned by CenterPoint Properties, leased to Boise Cascade. Airport East area.
7525-cogswell-st-romulus-mi-ice-warehous: ICE purchased warehouse in Romulus MI for 500-bed processing center without local notice. Michigan AG and city sued to block; 700+ protesters rallied.
8251-liberty-rd-harris-tx-430000-sf: 430K SF spec warehouse in Houston, Harris County TX, completed 2025, $25M investment by First Industrial Realty Trust. Currently available.
